Na0.35CoO2.1.3H2O.name: Na0.35CoO2.1.3H2O
Na0.35CoO2.1.3H2O.notes: Already has water intercalation. Triangular Co lattice frustrates AF. Low Tc reflects low DOS and weak coupling. Pairing likely d+id or f-wave on triangular lattice.

CsV3Sb5.name: CsV3Sb5
CsV3Sb5.notes: Kagome metal with CDW and SC. Frustration is geometric. Low Tc and weak correlations. Van Hove singularity near Fermi level. Pairing mechanism debated: electron-phonon vs unconventional.

KV3Sb5.name: KV3Sb5
KV3Sb5.notes: Lower Tc than CsV3Sb5. Same kagome vanadium structure.

Fe3Sn2.name: Fe3Sn2
Fe3Sn2.notes: Kagome ferromagnet. Not an AF system. Frustration acts on non-collinear spin states. No superconductivity observed.

Cd2Re2O7.name: Cd2Re2O7
Cd2Re2O7.notes: Pyrochlore superconductor. Very low Tc = 1 K. Strong SOC from Re 5d. Frustration present but correlations weak. Likely conventional s-wave pairing.

kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu2(CN)3.name: kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu2(CN)3
kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu2(CN)3.notes: Organic Mott insulator on anisotropic triangular lattice. Spin liquid ground state (no AF order). SC under pressure ~4 K. Pairing likely d-wave or d+id. Very low energy scales.

kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu[N(CN)2]Br.name: kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu[N(CN)2]Br
kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu[N(CN)2]Br.notes: Higher-Tc organic on less frustrated triangular lattice (t'/t ~ 0.7). SC at ambient pressure. d-wave-like nodal gap observed.

NaNiO2.name: NaNiO2
NaNiO2.notes: Triangular Ni lattice. Correlated but insulating. Not superconducting. Possible d-wave with doping.

Herbertsmithite ZnCu3(OH)6Cl2.name: Herbertsmithite ZnCu3(OH)6Cl2
Herbertsmithite ZnCu3(OH)6Cl2.notes: Prototypical kagome spin liquid. J ~ 170 K but T_N = 0. Insulating: cannot superconduct without extreme doping. Demonstrates frustration kills AF order but material is insulating.

Nd2Ir2O7.name: Nd2Ir2O7
Nd2Ir2O7.notes: Pyrochlore iridate. Strong SOC. Insulating with all-in-all-out magnetic order. Not superconducting. Weyl semimetal physics near transition.
